codeReview.summary: The feature stays scoped to readiness hardening: it adds `/health` parity with `/ping`, exposes machine-readable readiness booleans in runtime diagnostics, and strengthens regression coverage for validation overrides and zero-live completed-boot behavior. I found no correctness or scope issues in the committed diff.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The baseline endpoint smoke script still probes only `/ping` for readiness and does not exercise `/health`, even though the runtime-provider skill explicitly says readiness-probe features must reconcile README/service-manifest/testing guidance with the implemented probe surface in the same change set.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: scripts/test-endpoints.sh:205-210 waits for `/simple/price` readiness and then checks `GET /ping`, but contains no `/health` probe; runtime-provider-worker SKILL.md mission note says to reconcile README/service-manifest/testing guidance with the actual runtime probe contract when readiness probes change.

summary: Reviewed the worker transcript skeleton, handoff, and commit `603cec05ece3933de586479600a13db62592834a`. The implementation correctly hardens readiness surfaces without widening scope, and the added tests cover the newly exposed readiness booleans and `/health` parity; no blocking or non-blocking code defects were found.
